file.content:
A node failed to rejoin the cluster after a restart.

Restore full membership while keeping the cluster healthy.

Constraints:
- Do not install operators or CRDs.
- Keep the cluster healthy.

Verification:
1. Cluster health is yellow/green.
2. All nodes appear in /_cat/nodes.
3. app-data remains readable.

content.thinking:
I can see the precondition log. It seems:
1. A 3-node Elasticsearch cluster was set up
2. The ConfigMap was deliberately corrupted with wrong seed hosts (using `default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`)
3. Then it scaled down to 2 replicas, deleted `es-cluster-2`'s PVC, and scaled back up to 3
4. Waited for only 2 nodes to be visible (confirming es-cluster-2 can't rejoin due to wrong seed hosts)

So the scenario is: es-cluster-2 fails to rejoin because the `elasticsearch.yml` ConfigMap has wrong seed hosts pointing to `default` namespace instead of `elasticsearch` namespace.
Now let me verify the current state of the cluster to see what we're working with.

content.text:
Now I understand the setup. The precondition corrupted the ConfigMap with wrong seed hosts (`default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`) and then forced es-cluster-2 to restart without its PVC, leaving it unable to rejoin.

Let me check the current cluster state.

kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -o wide

content.thinking:
Confirmed: es-cluster-2 is in Error state, and only 2 nodes (es-cluster-0 and es-cluster-1) appear in `/_cat/nodes`. 

The issue is the ConfigMap `es-config` has wrong seed hosts. The seed hosts point to `default.svc.cluster.local` namespace, but the correct namespace is `elasticsearch`. 

Let me check the current configmap and the logs of es-cluster-2 to confirm.

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r7/elasticsearch-config-drift-recovery-20260703_103149/bundle/kubeconfig

	at org.elasticsearch.cli.EnvironmentAwareCommand.createEnv(EnvironmentAwareCommand.java:82)
	at org.elasticsearch.cli.EnvironmentAwareCommand.execute(EnvironmentAwareCommand.java:77)
	at org.elasticsearch.cli.Command.mainWithoutErrorHandling(Command.java:112)
	at org.elasticsearch.cli.Command.main(Command.java:77)
	at org.elasticsearch.bootstrap.Elasticsearch.main(Elasticsearch.java:125)
	at org.elasticsearch.bootstrap.Elasticsearch.main(Elasticsearch.java:80)

content.thinking:
The issue is clear. There are two problems with the ConfigMap:

1. **Wrong seed hosts**: Using `default.svc.cluster.local` instead of `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`
2. **Empty `node.name`**: The `node.name: ` is empty/blank, which is why es-cluster-2 fails with `null-valued setting found for key [node.name]`

The running nodes (es-cluster-0 and es-cluster-1) must have gotten the correct `node.name` from somewhere else - likely from the `POD_NAME` environment variable being substituted, but now the ConfigMap has an empty `node.name` value.
The `${POD_NAME}` variable substitution in the ConfigMap appears to have failed, leaving `node.name` empty when es-cluster-2 tried to start. The earlier pods might have been running with a previous configuration or obtained their node names through a different mechanism. I need to check how the StatefulSet injects environment variables into the pods to understand where the node name should actually come from. The new pods will pick up the broken ConfigMap when they restart, so I need to fix it first with the correct variable substitution and seed hosts, then carefully restart es-cluster-2 without losing cluster quorum. Let me check how the ConfigMap is mounted in the StatefulSet to understand the restart strategy.

content.text: Two issues found: the ConfigMap has (1) wrong seed hosts (`default` namespace instead of `elasticsearch`) and (2) empty `node.name` (the `${POD_NAME}` env var was shell-expanded to empty). Let me check the StatefulSet to understand how node names are normally set.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r7/elasticsearch-config-drift-recovery-20260703_103149/bundle/kubeconfig
kubectl -n elasticsearch get statefulset es-cluster -o yaml
input.description: Check StatefulSet definition to understand node.name injection
